commit_scope: Go native front-door upstream fanout bridge for unmatched Python routes

commands: cd form/form-kernel-go && gofmt -w server.go server_test.go && go test ./...

cd form/form-kernel-go && go run . serve --port 18182 --upstream https://api.coherencycoin.com --stdlib ../form-stdlib --config /Users/ursmuff/.coherence-network/secrets/form-kernel-postgres-tunnel.json ../../deploy/front-door/api.bml
local_validation.summary: The Go native front door now accepts --upstream and explicitly fans out unmatched requests to a Python/FastAPI upstream while stamping X-Form-Router: fanout-python. Native routes still return X-Form-Router: native-kernel-go. This makes Python a bridge behind the kernel listener without claiming bridged endpoints as high-grammar native.

ci_validation.reason: No commit or PR created in this turn.

deploy_validation.reason: No production deploy performed. The bridge was validated locally against the public production API as upstream.

phase_gate.reason: Local tests and curl proof passed, but CI/deploy validation has not run for this uncommitted worktree.

evidence_refs: Go test proof: cd form/form-kernel-go && go test ./... passed, including TestUpstreamURLPreservesPathAndQuery and TestFanoutForwardsBodyAndMarksBridge.
Native route proof: local Go listener returned HTTP/1.1 200 OK, X-Form-Router: native-kernel-go, and status=ok for GET /api/health.
Fanout proof: local Go listener returned HTTP/1.1 200 OK, X-Form-Router: fanout-python, kind=lattice, and lattice keys blueprints_total/cells_total/recipes_total for POST /api/substrate/form.
Architecture memory updated to state that fanout is an explicit bridge and does not count as high-grammar native promotion.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The Go kernel listener can be the single local front door while unmatched Python endpoints remain reachable as explicit fanout bridge traffic.
test_flows: matched BML route executes natively and stamps X-Form-Router: native-kernel-go
unmatched substrate form POST fans out to the configured upstream and stamps X-Form-Router: fanout-python
fanout preserves request path, query, body, and response body in Go unit tests
